page: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 40 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities but shows limitations under multi-threaded workloads. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and memory bandwidth tests falling into the average tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and overall PassMark CPU scores register in the weak category, reflecting the shared CPU allocation.

Because the CPU resources are shared, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated compute power. It is well-suited for lightweight applications such as static web serving, basic utility scripting, and small-model LLM inference prompt processing. It is less optimal for database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, or multi-threaded processing tasks, where its shared CPU allocation and lower multi-core benchmark scores present clear performance tradeoffs.
description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 40 GB SSD. While single-core benchmarks like stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression place this server in the top tier, its multi-core and memory performance fall into average and weak categories due to the shared CPU allocation. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for entry-level workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight application hosting, and basic development tasks, whereas resource-intensive database operations and multi-threaded processing represent clear performance tradeoffs.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB of RAM, and a 40 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance for entry-level workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with balanced memory and local SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 40 GB local SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
KVM hypervisor architecture with one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight applications, and development
